Richard Dabate told authorities his wife, Connie, was fatally shot by an obese man who had broken into the couple’s home in Connecticut at 9am one day. That ran counter to Connie’s Fitbit, which indicated she was in spinning class at the time.

Connie was posting videos to Facebook and sending friends messages from her home’s internet as late as 9:40am, according to data obtained by police, and her last registered movement on the FitBit was at 10:05am—evidence the cops used to justify arresting her husband.
He has since been charged with Connie’s murder, tampering with physical evidence, and making false statements to police.
You can select what alerts you want to receive. We’ll only buzz you when it’s really important; otherwise, your phone will just light up quietly. Enjoy.
